title: Secretarial Records Management in India | Corporate Compliance Consultant

hero.headline: Maintain Secretarial Records with Experts
hero.subtext: Legally compliant. Audit-ready. End-to-end record keeping by professionals.

mainHeading: Corporate Secretarial Records Management & Compliance in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Secretarial records are the significant statutory documents which are required to be maintained by the company under the Companies Act, 2013 and rules framed under such Act by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). These include registers, minutes, resolutions, notices and disclosures that evidence the company’s internal decision-making and regulatory compliance. All the secretarial records of the company need to be well maintained for Private Limited, Public Limited, One Person Companies (OPC) and Section 8 Companies. It covers drafting / scrutinising of various documents, minutes, reports, registers and other secretarial records and conduct periodic review of all secretarial records maintained by the Company as required under the Companies Act, 2013 and Secretarial Standards prescribed by ICSI and ensuring that you remain compliant and audit ready anytime during the year.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Maintaining secretarial records is a legal obligation for all companies under the Companies Act, 2013. These records reflect transparency, corporate governance and decision-making integrity. Non-maintenance of such records can lead to fines, disqualification of directors and other legal consequences during audits, inspections or litigation. Investors and financial institutions often demand updated secretarial documents during due diligence. Professionals Law makes sure your secretarial records are forever up-to-date and regulatory compliant and can be inspected in seconds thus relieving the pressure on in-house teams and protecting against regulatory issues.

body.title: Ensures Legal Compliance
body.description: Secretarial records demonstrate that the company adheres to the Companies Act, reducing legal exposure and enhancing governance.

body.title: Improves Corporate Governance
body.description: Maintaining accurate board minutes, resolutions and registers promotes transparency and responsible management practices.

body.title: Essential for Audits & Due Diligence
body.description: Updated records are required during statutory audits, investor reviews, M&A transactions and funding rounds.

body.title: Avoids Penalties & Disqualification
body.description: Professionals Law helps prevent penalties under the Companies Act by ensuring all records are timely and accurately maintained.

body.title: Supports Director Protection
body.description: Proper documentation helps directors prove compliance and decisions made in good faith, offering legal protection.

categories.applicantType: The following entities must maintain secretarial records:
criteria: Companies having paid-up capital ≥ ₹10 crore or turnover ≥ ₹50 crore (mandatory appointment of CS)
Companies required to follow Secretarial Standards (SS-1 & SS-2)

documents: Proof of Annual Filings with MCA

body.description: Professionals Law reviews your company’s structure and compliance requirements.
We check existing statutory records and identify any gaps or defaults.
Missing registers, resolutions and minutes are prepared and validated.
All records are aligned with Secretarial Standards and MCA requirements.
Professionals Law provides periodic updates and reminders to maintain compliance.

body.title: Failure to maintain secretarial records may result in:
body.description: Penalty of ₹50,000 to ₹3,00,000 on the company
Penalty of ₹50,000 and imprisonment or fine for officers in default
Director disqualification in case of continued non-compliance
Additional penalties for non-maintenance of specific registers under Rule 3 of the Companies (Management and Administration) Rules, 2014
Professionals Law helps avoid such liabilities through proactive and accurate record keeping.

body.question: What are secretarial records?
body.answer: These are statutory registers, meeting minutes, resolutions and disclosures maintained as per the Companies Act.

body.question: Is it mandatory for private companies?
body.answer: Yes, all companies, including private limited companies, must maintain secretarial records.

body.question: What if records are missing or lost?
body.answer: Professionals Law can help recreate missing documents using available company data and past filings.

body.question: How often should secretarial records be updated?
body.answer: They should be updated after every Board or General Meeting and annually before filing returns.

body.question: Who is responsible for maintaining these records?
body.answer: The Company Secretary or designated company officers are responsible. Professionals Law can act as your outsourced secretarial partner.

body.question: Are these records checked during audits?
body.answer: Yes, both statutory auditors and MCA inspectors review these documents during inspections.

body.question: Do startups need to maintain these too?
body.answer: Yes, even startups are required to maintain secretarial records to stay compliant.

body.question: Can Professionals Law provide secretarial services remotely?
body.answer: Yes, we offer end-to-end secretarial support both onsite and remotely across India.

body.question: How does Professionals Law ensure compliance?
body.answer: We use standardized templates, regular audits and expert legal oversight to ensure all records are compliant and up-to-date.
